chunks.text: Undergraduate Minors - EECS at Berkeley Undergraduate Minors The Computer Science Minor The minor program is offered through the College of Engineering and is open to all Berkeley undergraduates with a declared major other than EECS or CS. To be admitted to the CS minor, you must have acquired a cumulative grade point average of 2.0 in the required coursework. The Electrical Engineering & Computer Science Minor The EECS minor, offered through the College of Engineering, is an optional program for students declared in another major but interested in coherent EECS study. It is available to all eligible students, regardless of their college. Students interested in only studying computer science should consider the Computer Science Minor. The Electronic Intelligent Systems Minor The minor Electronic Intelligent Systems (EIS) offers the opportunity to gain breadth as well as depth in the area of electronic intelligent systems that connect to the physical and social world Resources Information for Current Students Broadening Participation in EECS Department Policy on Academic Misconduct Wellness Resources Student Concerns and Grievances Student Computing and Email Curricular Practical Training (CPT) Resources for Faculty and Staff Staff Contact Quick List Administrative Services Course and Instructional Support Academic Personnel Information Event Planning Calendar Undergraduate Faculty Advising – for Faculty Staff IT Procedures Graduate Student Review Information for Faculty My EECS Info Guide A Guide to IEEE and ACM Awards Academic Personnel Information Center for Student Affairs Visiting Research Scholars Prospective Visiting Scholar Information & Applications New Visiting Scholar Information Information for Current
